zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Empson於1320年出生在英國英格蘭時，她的父親Richard Empson45歲，母親Jane45歲。她於1352年與Sir William Barrett Of Chipperham在美國麻薩諸塞州米德萊可沃特托結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1368年在英國倫敦享年48歲。

nameMeanings.name: Empson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: metronymic meaning ‘son of Emma’, from the Middle English female personal name Em(e), Emme (see Imm ) + -son.
shortened form of Emerson . The two names were no doubt confused, and it is possible that the reverse process sometimes occurred, whereby Em(m)erson was an altered form of Emmeson, i.e. Em(p)son.

variants.name: Imeson
meanings: English: variant either of Emmeson (see Empson ) or of Emerson .

variants.name: Impson
meanings: English (Norfolk): variant of   Empson  . This form of the surname is now more common in the US than in Britain.

variants.name: Imm
meanings: German: from the ancient Germanic personal name Im(m)o, a pet form of names based on Irmin-, a divine or ethnic name formed with irmin, ermen ‘whole, entire’.

English (Wiltshire): from the ancient Germanic female personal name Imma, Emma, of the same origin as in 1 above. In Old English Imma, Emma was borne by both males and females. In Middle English, under Norman influence, the personal name Emm(a), Imm(a) came to be used almost exclusively for women, being taken as a short form of Ermingard.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Empson was born in 1320, in England, United Kingdom, her father, Richard Empson, was 45 and her mother, Jane, was 45. She married Sir William Barrett Of Chipperham in 1352, in Watertown, Middlesex, Massachusetts, United States. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1368, in London, England, at the age of 48.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Empson est née en 1320, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Richard Empson, avait 45 ans et sa mère, Jane, avait 45 ans. Elle a épousé Sir William Barrett Of Chipperham en 1352, à Watertown, Middlesex, Massachusetts, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1368, à Londres, Angleterre, à l’âge de 48 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane Empson 1320 in England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Richard Empson 45 und ihre Mutter Jane 45. Sie hat 1352 in Watertown, Middlesex, Massachusetts, Vereinigte Staaten Sir William Barrett Of Chipperham geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1368 im Alter von 48 Jahren in London, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Empson(이)가 1320년 영국 잉글랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard Empson(은)는 45세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Jane(은)는 45세였다. 그녀는 1352년 미국 매사추세츠 주 미들레세스 왓르타운에서 Sir William Barrett Of Chipperham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1368년 영국 런던에서 48세의 나이로 사망했다.
